channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vancouver, B.C. Government and First Nations Partner to Retain Whitecaps in the City Long-Term
item.description: Representatives from the Vancouver Whitecaps, Major League Soccer, the City of Vancouver, the B.C. provincial government, the federal government, PavCo, and three local First Nations — Musqueam, Squam...

item.title: Benin City Residents Turn to Traditional Medicine Amid High Healthcare Costs
item.description: People for Benin City, Edo State don dey struggle to manage healthcare as the price of medicine don waka pass normal. Many residents dey complain say because drug prices, hospital fees, and lab charge...

item.title: British Gas Faces £163.2 Million Compensation for Prepayment Meter Customers
item.description: British Gas, one of the UK's largest energy suppliers, has agreed to pay £163.2 million as compensation to customers who were affected by unfair practices related to prepayment meters. This settlement...

item.title: Trump wraps up China visit, discusses trade, AI and regional tensions with Xi
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has wrapped up his official visit to China, saying he secured important trade deals with Chinese President Xi Jinping. During his visit to Beijing, Trump aimed to ...

item.title: Western Cape Minibus Taxi Fares Set to Increase Amid Rising Costs
item.description: Minibus taxi fares in the Western Cape are set to rise, causing concern among commuters already struggling with financial pressures. Local trips will see fare increases ranging from R2 to R5, while lo...

item.title: Trump and Xi Discuss Taiwan, Trade, and Security Concerns in Strategic Summit
item.description: The recent summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping addressed several critical global issues, including trade, technology, the conflict in Iran, and maintaining the op...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif Highlights Strong Pak-China Ties in Meeting with Chinese Tech Delegation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif warmly welcomed a high-level Chinese business delegation in Islamabad on Friday and praised the historic and all-weather strategic partnership between Pakistan and China....

item.title: Secret Service Standoff with Chinese Officials During Trump's Beijing Visit
item.description: During President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing on May 14, 2026, tensions escalated when Chinese officials attempted to disarm a U.S. Secret Service agent outside the historic Temple of Heaven. Accor...

item.title: France Announce 26-Man Squad for 2026 World Cup, Mbappe to Lead
item.description: France don announce their 26-man squad wey go represent dem for the 2026 World Cup wey go hold for United States, Canada, and Mexico. Kylian Mbappe go dey captain the team even though e still dey reco...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of MGM's 1949 Adaptation of Little Women
item.description: The 1949 adaptation of Louisa May Alcott’s classic novel 'Little Women' was a grand Technicolor production by Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM), directed and produced by Mervyn LeRoy as part of MGM’s 25th-ann...

item.title: Andy Burnham cleared to stand in Makerfield as Labour leadership tensions rise
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer will not prevent Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham from contesting the Makerfield parliamentary seat, following the decision of sitting MP Josh Simons to step down. Burnham intend...

item.title: Southern Manitoba experiences rare dusty rain during strong winds
item.description: Southern Manitoba experienced an unusual weather event on May 14, 2026, when a storm combined rain with airborne dust, resulting in 'dirty rain' that left vehicles and surfaces coated in a layer of gr...

item.title: Tara Pavlovic Embraces Single Life and Personal Growth After Separation
item.description: Tara Pavlovic, former star of The Bachelor, is navigating life post-separation and finding unexpected joy in her newfound independence. Recently separated from her husband and father of her two young ...

item.title: Guide to the 11 US Host Cities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is set to be a massive event, spanning eleven major US cities across three time zones. South Africans keen to swap the winter chill for an American summer should ensure their B...

item.title: Kenya Maintains Somalia Border Closure Amid Security Concerns
item.description: Kenya has decided to keep its border with Somalia closed due to renewed instability in the neighbouring country, President William Ruto announced during the Africa Champions Summit in Nairobi. Initial...

item.title: Parliamentary Panel Favours Pen-and-Paper Mode for NEET-UG Exams Amid Concerns on Exam Security
item.description: Prior to the cancellation of the NEET-UG examination due to a paper leak, the Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education, Women, Children, Youth and Sports had recommended maintaining the tradition...

item.title: Experts question the Opposition's housing and migration policies
item.description: The Opposition has proposed linking migration levels to housing construction, suggesting Australia should only accept one migrant per new home built. Their plan includes redirecting funds from existin...

item.title: Congress Criticises Modi Government Over Request for US Waiver on Russian Oil Imports
item.description: The Congress party on Friday strongly criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi, claiming that the government is compromising India’s honour and energy security. This comes after reports that India has ...

item.title: Candid Photobooth Portraits Reveal the Lives of Young Women in the 1920s
item.description: In the 1920s, the invention of the automated photobooth by Anatol Josepho transformed how people captured personal images. Moving away from the formal, rigid studio photography of the Victorian era, p...

item.title: Preston players transfer to Lower Plenty in DVCA offseason
item.description: The article could not be accessed due to website restrictions. News Corp Australia detected crawler bot activity and blocked access. Readers or subscribers who are not bots are advised to check their ...
